description_long: This object is a large, curved structure resembling a natural claw or pincer, with a hard, smooth exterior and a slightly rougher, textured underside. The surface coloration is primarily a pale beige or yellowish tone, with dark purplish-black patches and a faint reddish tint near the tip. The object features a prominent, hooked upper section and a matching lower section, both tapering to points and joined at a visible hinge-like joint. The interior reveals some bony or shell-like structural details, suggesting a robust, organic construction. The overall form is elongated and slightly flattened, with a naturalistic, weathered appearance. There are no visible markings, illustrations, or text on the surface.
description: A large, curved, claw-like object with a hard, pale exterior and dark patches, featuring a prominent hooked tip and a visible joint. The surface is smooth with some rough, naturalistic textures.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved, claw-like object with visible joint and color gradation from pale beige to dark tip.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the thickness and curvature, with the dark patch on the underside more prominent.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the matching curvature and joint, with more visible interior structural details.
description_view_3: View from the base, showing the blunt end and the convergence of the upper and lower sections.
